reasoning: Initial observation suggests a direct relationship between mass, density, volume, and k. Increasing k will help us determine its role in the equation.
Doubling k from 3 to 4 increased the mass from 60 to 80, suggesting k directly affects the mass. Next, I will increase the volume to observe its effect.
The mass has quadrupled when both k and volume were doubled, indicating a linear relationship with volume and k. Further experimentation is needed to confirm the exact equation.
The mass remains unchanged when k is decreased, suggesting k does not affect the mass independently. Given the relationships observed, the equation likely involves density and volume directly.
